title: John W. Boscariol
content:
Partner | Co-Head, International Trade and Investment Law
People.ContactEmail jboscariol@mccarthy.ca
- University of Toronto
With unmatched knowledge of international trade and investment law and policy, John is widely recognized as one of Canada’s foremost experts in the space.
John Boscariol is Co-Head, International Trade and Investment Law Group and a Partner in our Business Law Group. Having guided clients through the world’s most challenging recent global trade events, he has become indispensable in protecting and expanding clients’ access to foreign markets, especially during geopolitical conflict, trade wars, and tariff disputes. John further helps businesses manage their operations and supply chains in compliance with evolving anti-modern slavery regimes, economic sanctions, export and technology transfer controls, as well as anti-terrorism and anti-corruption laws, among other economic and national security measures.
John’s expertise in international trade and investment continues to be recognized by the most notable directories, including Band 1 rankings from both Chambers Global and Chambers Canada. He is also recipient of the Canadian Bar Association’s Award of Excellence in International Law. When trade challenges become front-page news, John’s expert opinion is highly sought after by mainstream media. He is frequently quoted and interviewed in the Wall Street Journal, CBC, Globe and Mail, and BNN Bloomberg, evidencing his ability to cut to the heart of a matter and accurately anticipate where markets will move.

#### International trade and investment expertise
With more than 25 years of experience in international trade and investment law, John helps clients maintain perspective when facing the day’s biggest trade challenges. His understanding of the interaction of Canadian regulatory regimes with those of the United States, the European Union and the United Kingdom distinguish him as a true expert in cross-border commerce in some of the largest and most dynamic markets. John combines this technical expertise with a deep understanding of his clients’ industries, making him a trusted business partner to decision makers in the technology, defence, energy, mining, manufacturing, consumer products, aerospace, transport, and financial services sectors.
As global economic and national security frameworks evolve, John prioritizes safeguarding organizations' trade and investment goals by deftly navigating new tariffs, sanctions, export and import controls, government procurement, and trade remedies. His strong relationships with Canadian government agencies make him the go-to person for clients needing expert guidance when dealing with regulators, investigators, and policy makers.

#### Leadership in the legal profession
John is currently Co-Chair of the American Bar Association Export Controls and Economic Sanctions Committee. He is Past Chair of the Export Committee of the Canadian Association of Importers and Exporters and is active in Canadian bar associations, including as a member of the Canadian Bar Association’s Joint Committee on Anti-Corruption and as Past Chair of the Ontario Bar Association International Law Section.
John is an adjunct professor at the Faculty of Law at the University of Western Ontario, where he designed and teaches Anti-Corruption Law and its Application in International Business . He also lectures on a broad range of international trade and investment law topics at other law schools, including at the universities of Toronto, Ottawa, Windsor and Calgary and at Osgoode Hall Law School.
In addition to his frequent conversations with Canadian business and political reporters, John speaks at industry and professional events across North America. He has contributed to more than 200 recent publications, attributions, and conference presentations.
John also testifies before Canada's Parliament on the enforcement of economic sanctions and anti-terrorism laws, where many of his recommendations have been adopted, including in the Senate Report of the Standing Committee on Foreign Affairs and International Trade: Strengthening Canada’s Autonomous Sanctions Architecture: Five-Year Legislative Review of the Sergei Magnitsky Law and the Special Economic Measures Act (2023), and in the House of Commons Report of the Standing Committee on Foreign Affairs: A Coherent and Effective Approach to Canada’s Sanctions Regimes: Sergei Magnitsky and Beyond (2017).
John received his B. Comm. (Gold Medalist) from the University of Toronto in 1990. He received his LLB from the Faculty of Law at the University of Toronto and was called to the Ontario bar in 1995. In 1998, he received his LLM (International Trade and Competition Law) from the Osgoode Hall Law School.
